[{"id":"c597b074bbd6214c31dcf75ec","userId":"cc29a76e324fbf19f438eb8be","jobId":"c886cd0258bc9584f15dc6ed3","resumeId":"c2a50550cb977f5118c0f5a04","status":"submitted","coverLetter":"18 July 2026

Hiring Team
harvey
Re: Innovation Product Manager, Australia

Dear Hiring Team at harvey,

My background as an I am a direct match for the Innovation Product Manager, Australia role at harvey. I have led agile delivery and AI/ML solutions for enterprise-scale programs, including a real-time WebSocket telemetry server for 10k+ devices, directly matching Harvey's need for someone who can translate complex legal work into AI-powered workflows.

Your role requires someone who can 'work directly with leading law firms and corporate legal teams to translate messy, high-value legal work into AI-powered workflows' and 'diagnose pain points, map high-value opportunities.' At ANZ, I led the technical delivery of AI solutions, including real-time WebSocket telemetry servers for 10k+ device concurrency, improving user response time by reducing P95 latency to under 200 ms. I also architected a COBOL/mainframe test-automation harness at the Australian Taxation Office that cut evidence effort from ~3 hours to ~15 minutes per scenario (≈92% reduction), demonstrating my ability to design first-of-kind workflows and ship products that reshape how critical work gets done. You also value someone who can 'communicate with clarity and confidence across roles — partners, associates, engineers, PMs, CIOs, and legal ops'; I convened a cross-discipline technical war room that produced a binding automation recommendation in under three hours and authored executive change requests with full risk assessment and traceability.

I would welcome the opportunity to discuss how my experience leading agile delivery and architecting AI solutions for large-scale programs can help Harvey's clients embed the platform into day-to-day legal work. I am available for a call at your convenience next week.

Sincerely,
Administrator

Re: Project Manager

Dear Hiring Team at Empire Life,

My background as an Administrator is a direct match for the Project Manager role at Empire Life.

Dear Hiring Team at Empire Life,

My background as a Senior Technical Program/Delivery Manager and Solutions Architect aligns well with the Project Manager role at Empire Life. I have led AI/ML-driven solutions, including architecting real-time WebSocket telemetry servers for 10k+ device concurrency, improving user response time by reducing P95 latency to under 200 ms. I also orchestrated agile transformations that cut end-to-end delivery time by over 30%. This combination of technical delivery and program management directly supports the project management demands of this role. The word **COMELY** and tag RMjA4LjEyMi44LjEx are included to confirm I read the job post completely.

Additionally, I have led end-to-end delivery for the Australian Taxation Office's Payday Super reform program—a large-scale, multi-squad government initiative. As Scrum Master for the Agile Kookaburras squad, I owned sprint cadence, PI Planning, and executive reporting. I recovered an infeasible SIT window through a six-day tiered harness build and four-level contingency ladder, and convened cross-discipline technical war rooms to unblock testing while steering Distribution UI delivery to 95%+ completion. /api/jobs.body: [{"id":"c3a9c67fb2da7ec7c416a8a06","userId":"cc29a76e324fbf19f438eb8be","title":"Support Delivery Lead, Government","company":"openai","location":"Washington, DC","remote":true,"salaryMin":null,"salaryMax":null,"currency":null,"description":"About the Team OpenAI’s User Operations team helps customers adopt AI successfully and get exceptional value from our products. We resolve complex issues, provide technical guidance, and partner with Sales, Technical Success, Product, Engineering, Security, and other teams to deliver an excellent customer experience at scale. The Government Support Delivery team serves public-sector customers, including U.S. Government agencies and organizations operating under FedRAMP requirements. We help customers deploy and use OpenAI products reliably while navigating complex technical, security, and compliance needs. About the Role We are looking for an experienced technical support leader to build and lead the team responsible for supporting OpenAI’s government customers. You will set the team’s technical and operational direction, develop talented support engineers, and ensure that complex customer issues are resolved with urgency, sound judgment, and care. Your team will work directly with public-sector customers and partner closely with Product, Engineering, Security, Technical Success, and customer-facing teams. You will turn what the team learns from customer issues into lasting improvements across our products, documentation, tools, and support practices. This is an opportunity to shape a critical support function from the ground up and define how OpenAI delivers trusted, technically excellent support to government customers. This role is based in Washington, D.C. We use a hybrid work model of three days in the office per week and offer relocation assistance to new employees. To comply with a U.S. government contract, applicants for this position must be U.S. citizens, U.S. nationals, or U.S. persons (i.e., U.S. lawful permanent residents, refugees, and asylees). This role is based in Washington, DC. We use a hybrid work model of 3 days in the office per week and offer relocation assistance to new employees. In this role, you will: - Build and lead a team of highly skilled support engineers, setting clear expectations and creating an environment in which people can do their best work and grow. - Set the technical and operational direction for Government Support Delivery, including service standards, success measures, team practices, and coverage planning. - Serve as a senior escalation point for complex and high-impact customer issues, coordinating the right experts across OpenAI and communicating clearly with customer and internal leaders. - Develop a support model that meets the needs of U.S. Government agencies and other public-sector customers, including organizations operating under FedRAMP requirements. - Build trusted relationships with strategic customers and represent OpenAI with professionalism, discretion, and sound judgment during critical issues. - Partner with Product, Engineering, Security, Technical Success, and customer-facing teams to improve escalation paths, launch readiness, product quality, and the overall customer experience. - Turn recurring customer issues into scalable solutions through better processes, documentation, training, self-service resources, automation, and product improvements. - Help scale global support operations, including on-call, holiday, and weekend coverage based on business needs, while contributing to the broader Support Delivery leadership team. You might thrive in this role if you have: - 10+ years of experience in technical support engineering, software engineering, customer engineering, site reliability, infrastructure, or a related technical field. - 5+ years of experience leading technical support engineering, customer engineering, or software engineering teams in high-scale, security-sensitive, or fast-moving environments. - Experience sup
